mechanism_narrative: A low and stable federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary policy, reducing the cost of capital and supporting equity valuations, which leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5% for 6 consecutive months, but SPX 20d returns are consistently negative or flat (average return < 0.1%) over that period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, indicating an economic slowdown that will negatively impact corporate earnings and lead to lower S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over any 3-month period, but SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 50% of such triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with robust payroll employment growth indicates healthy consumer demand and a strong economy, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0.5% AND PAYEMS MoM growth > 150k
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and PAYEMS show strong growth as specified, but SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered periods over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating GDP growth, investor confidence increases, leading to higher valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon, as future earnings expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth accelerates by > 0.5pp
failure_condition: If GDP growth accelerates in a recovery/expansion regime, but SPX 60d returns are consistently negative (average return < 0%) over a 1-year period of such triggers.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp and unexpected rise in inflation (CPIAUCSL) above a certain threshold signals potential central bank tightening, which can lead to higher discount rates and negative short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors price in rate hikes.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL MoM growth > 0.4% AND YoY growth > 3.0%
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL crosses the specified thresholds, but SPX 5d returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production indicates a healthy manufacturing sector and overall economic expansion, supporting corporate earnings and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows consistent growth (average MoM > 0.1%) but SPX 20d returns are flat or negative (average return < 0%) over a sustained period of 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts coupled with low federal funds rates indicates strong housing demand supported by accommodative monetary policy, boosting construction-related sectors and overall economic activity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If housing starts are strong and FEDFUNDS is low as specified, but SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 40% of such periods over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of very strong labor market growth (high PAYEMS), the market may anticipate inflationary pressures and subsequent monetary tightening, leading to short-term negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth exceeds 300k in a late-cycle expansion, but SPX 5d returns are consistently positive (average return > 0.1%) over a 6-month period of such triggers.
